title: crepis vesicaria subsp. andryaloides
intro: Crepis vesicaria subsp. andryaloides, also known as Andry's hawksbeard, is a subspecies of plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Crepis vesicaria subsp. andryaloides can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used in tinctures and salves, making it a versatile remedy in traditional medicine.
